2026-03-06.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record highs
2026-03-06.exit_case: none - Strait remains actively blocked, tanker rates still at record highs, crisis ongoing since Feb 28
2026-03-06.assessment: Entry week. Iran's blockade of Hormuz since Feb 28 following US-Israeli strikes has driven VLCC rates to an all-time high ($423,736/day). This is a live, acute, still-unfolding supply shock with a clean pure-play vehicle (BWET) explicitly named by press as the under-owned way to express it. Catalyst is fresh, specific, and datable, with no resolution in sight - blockade is ongoing, not a one-off event that has passed.
2026-03-06.exit_advice: Hold BWET; exit only if Strait reopens or blockade lifted, not on quiet news weeks

2026-03-13.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record highs
2026-03-13.exit_case: none - Hormuz blockade remains active with tanker rates still elevated; no reopening reported
2026-03-13.assessment: No fresh coverage this week specifically on Hormuz blockade or tanker rates - the only news item is unrelated Pakistani equity market rally. This is silence on the core trend, not resolution. Blockade appears to remain in force (no reopening reported), so catalyst_resolved stays false and thesis_live remains true. However, per silence decay rule, stepping conviction down from prior implicit high level given lack of fresh trend-confirming press this week. Still an acute, live, unresolved supply shock - continue holding pure-play tanker exposure, but flag that continued silence will further compound the fade.
2026-03-13.exit_advice: Hold BWET; watch for any diplomatic resolution or blockade lift as hard exit trigger

2026-03-20.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record highs
2026-03-20.exit_case: none - blockade still active, oil hit fresh highs after Kharg Island strike, no reopening reported
2026-03-20.assessment: Fresh press this week directly ties oil price action and market attention to Strait of Hormuz developments, with escalation (US bombing of Kharg Island, a major Iranian oil hub) driving oil higher before easing. This is not resolution - it's escalation of the same acute supply shock. FTSE energy stocks rallying on Hormuz-linked developments confirms the trend remains live and market-relevant. Reset conviction upward from last week's silence-decayed level given this fresh trend confirmation, though below entry-week peak as the story is now well-covered in mainstream financial press rather than under-the-radar.
2026-03-20.exit_advice: Hold BWET; escalation (Kharg Island bombing) confirms conflict deepening not resolving - fresh coverage resets silence decay upward this week

2026-03-27.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record highs
2026-03-27.exit_case: none - no news this week confirms Hormuz reopening or blockade lifted; irrelevant construction spending story
2026-03-27.assessment: This week's only news item (US construction spending) is entirely unrelated to the Strait of Hormuz/tanker rate thesis - pure silence on the core trend. No reports of blockade lifting, no ceasefire, no reopening of the Strait. Per silence decay rule, stepping conviction down further from last week's escalation-driven reset since there is zero fresh confirmation this week. The driving condition (Iran's blockade, elevated VLCC rates) is presumed to remain in force absent any contrary reporting - this is an acute but still-unresolved supply shock, not staleness-as-exit. Continue holding pure-play tanker exposure but flag that a second consecutive quiet week compounds the fade; any further silence risks pushing this toward cull territory even though the position remains technically live.
2026-03-27.exit_advice: Hold BWET while blockade remains in force; watch for any reopening/de-escalation news which would flip exit_case to true. Continue trimming conviction on silence.

2026-04-03.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record highs
2026-04-03.exit_case: Trump reportedly open to ending war without reopening Hormuz - suggests war winding down, but blockade explicitly persists even in that scenario, so no reversal yet
2026-04-03.assessment: Third consecutive week of thin/indirect coverage on the core tanker-rate trend, but this week's item is directly Hormuz-relevant (unlike the two prior unrelated fillers), so conviction resets modestly upward from the silence-decayed trough rather than compounding further down. The headline itself is double-edged: it signals potential eventual war resolution (a long-run risk to the thesis) but explicitly carves out that Hormuz reopening is NOT included, meaning the acute supply shock (record VLCC rates, blocked Strait) persists for now. No blockade lifting, no reopening, no ceasefire covering the Strait itself - driving condition remains in force. Maintain hold, monitor for any headline that ties an actual Strait reopening to a deal, which would be the genuine resolution trigger.
2026-04-03.exit_advice: Hold BWET - fresh Hormuz-specific headline this week confirms blockade remains the active condition even as war-ending talks surface; the report explicitly notes reopening is NOT part of any deal being discussed, so the driving condition (blocked Strait, elevated VLCC rates) stays intact. This is not resolution - it's a signal that political dynamics may be shifting, but the physical chokepoint remains shut. Watch closely: if a future deal explicitly includes reopening the Strait, that would be the hard exit trigger. For now, continue holding the pure-play tanker vehicle.

2026-04-10.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record highs
2026-04-10.exit_case: US-Iran agreed two-week ceasefire; crude fell on expectations Strait supply normalizes, reversing blockade
2026-04-10.assessment: This week's news is the clearest resolution signal since entry: a US-Iran ceasefire was reached (2026-04-08), with crude falling on expectations that Strait of Hormuz energy supplies would resume flowing normally. This is not silence - it is the actual reversal of the driving condition (blockade/record VLCC rates) that the thesis was built on. Even though earlier in the week Iran 'showed no signs of relenting' and a Trump deadline loomed, the ceasefire outcome that materialized is precisely the kind of resolution event the exit rules require: the acute supply shock (blocked Strait, record tanker rates) is being priced out by markets, not escalated. While a two-week ceasefire could theoretically break down and reopen the trade, per the rules I must act on what has occurred, not speculate on a re-escalation. The catalyst has resolved - shipping/tanker rate premium tied to a blocked Strait is no longer the live driver.
2026-04-10.exit_advice: Exit BWET - the ceasefire directly reverses the driving condition (blockade/record tanker rates); market already pricing normalized Strait flows and lower crude, killing the acute supply-shock thesis even if the ceasefire is only two weeks and could later break down
